text: I know that this is exactly where I need to be.
I think that I have really underestimated the importance of the scriptures in my life, reading through various parts of the Book of Mormon have really awakened my spiritual senses.
God loves us more than we can fathom. It's pretty simple but I think that we sometimes push it to the side; we literally just can't understand how much we are loved!
This week has been pretty wild, and although a large portion of it was taken up just by traveling, a lot really did happen that was noteworthy.

I'd like you to share something uplifting with someone you've interacted with recently. You don't need to share something specific to the Gospel, and the person you share it with doesn't even have to be having a hard time, but I believe that this is a good thing to do.
source: Elder Killian's Uber Lengthy Email Edition Deluxe

text: There is a way to find every good thing, and that is by turning unto Christ. We can find good things in all sorts of ways but as we turn unto Christ for guidance, its going to be a lot easier, and the good will be so much greater!
God hears our prayers and eases our burdens according to our faith and willingness. I know that he loves each and every one of you.

I showed them the picture I made of Jesus's Tomb after his resurrection and explained to them a bit of that story. They said that even before they knew what the drawing was that they could feel something looking at it.
Now that I'm halfway through my mission, I can't go back and change the first year, but this next year is in my control; I want to make the most of it.
I feel like I am a much more humble and understanding person than before my mission, I'm so grateful for the experiences that have led me to the 'Me' I am today.
I know that God loves each of us, and that Jesus Christ loves us too. He is our Savior! Isn't that a huge miracle?!
We are doing our best, that's all that the Lord requires! He will provide those who seek Him in His own time, as long as we keep working hard!

It's been a really really really hard week for me emotionally due to some stuff I won't go into. But look at all of these great experiences and miracles even through the hard times. I'm so so grateful for God's hand in my life.
As long as I am pressing forward with the desire to do good, and love those around me, I know I will make it through whatever comes my way.
Choose the hard right over the easy wrong. Guess what, it's gonna be hard! But it's 1000000% worth it, thanks to the Savior, Jesus Christ.

One thing I focused on this past week was looking at those around me as the Savior would. There's nobody you hate, nobody that you view as less talented, there isn't any kind of preconception based on appearance or situation. It's only reaching out in love, and with Christlike confidence.
We can be confident if we know our actions are backed by Charity, Christlike love.

Having faith in Him before he was even born must have been a big spiritual challenge for the people at the time. But many people softened their hearts, followed the spirit, and received a testimony of Christ. And guess what! He came around. He's still here and He's here for each of us.

I said 'where does God need us to go down there?' And my companion turned to me and said he remembered a member lived down there who we could visit. On the way, we saw an old lady working in a field. I felt like I should give some art to her, so we stepped over some frogs and handed her a drawing I'd made.
I'm trying to gain a stronger desire to follow the Savior and learn more about him.
